content.text: That existing entry is for a *different* project (`qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai`, the shared POP DAM/CRM DB) — so a dedicated Oracle entry is warranted, and I'll mirror its notes style. I'll store the URL **accurately labeled as `oracle.old`**, since that's factually what `db.vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp.supabase.co` is.

input.title: Supabase DB Direct URL — The Oracle (oracle.old, vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp)
input.notes:
Direct Postgres connection string (DIRECT_URL) for The Oracle's OLD Supabase project. The "password" field on this item IS the full connection string.

⚠️ THIS IS oracle.old — the DECOMMISSIONED Ohio project (project ref vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp, region us-east-2). It is NOT current production. Current production for The Oracle is the "theoracle" project, ref eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg (N. Virginia / us-east-1), after the 2026-06-20 cutover. See C:\repos\oracle\AGENTS.md §7 ("Current Supabase project") and docs/deployment.md "Supabase project cutover checklist". Production migrations MUST use the eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg connection string, which is NOT stored in this item.

WHAT THIS IS
- The non-pooled "direct" connection: host db.<ref>.supabase.co, port 5432 — the right one for running migrations / DDL.
- The app runtime uses the POOLED connection instead (pooler host, port 6543); that is a different URL, not this one.
- The password embedded in the URL is the Oracle project's Postgres database password.

WHERE IT'S USED
- Repo: C:\repos\oracle (The Oracle monorepo).
- C:\repos\oracle\.env.local currently sets DATABASE_URL + DIRECT_URL to THIS project (oracle.old), so local dev and `pnpm db:migrate` target oracle.old unless overridden.
- The migration runner reads DIRECT_URL (preferred) or DATABASE_URL: packages/db/src/migrate.ts.

HOW FUTURE AI SESSIONS SHOULD USE IT
1. Read this item's password field (the full connection string).
2. To migrate THIS (old) DB from C:\repos\oracle:
     DIRECT_URL='<value from this item>' corepack pnpm db:migrate
   `pnpm db:migrate` runs: Drizzle journal migrations + all hand-written packages/db/migrations/sql/*.sql + seed (all idempotent).
3. Ship schema changes through `pnpm db:migrate` ONLY — never apply generated Drizzle 0*.sql via Supabase MCP apply_migration or drizzle-kit push (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md). Hand-written idempotent SQL may be applied via Supabase MCP.
4. For CURRENT PRODUCTION migrations, do NOT use this URL — obtain the eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg DIRECT_URL instead.

NOT: this is not the Supabase anon key, service-role key, dashboard login password, or OAuth secret — it is the direct Postgres connection string.

Stored 2026-06-24 at Albert's request. Related item in this vault: "Supabase DB Password - shared POP database" (a DIFFERENT project, qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai — popdam/CRM/PIM/DAM/PLM, not The Oracle).

Migration **succeeded against production**. The key lines:
- `applied: …75_extraction_status_awaiting_approval.sql`
- `applied: …76_transcript_approval.sql` (with the expected "constraint … does not exist, skipping" notice — that's the idempotent `DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS` on first run)
- `Migrations + seed complete.`

The many `relation … already exists, skipping` NOTICEs are the earlier hand-written SQL files re-applying idempotently — harmless and expected. So prod now has the `awaiting_approval` enum value and the `raw_transcripts` approval columns (with existing transcripts backfilled to `approved`).

content.text:
Two separate things — the CLI fix (quick) and the missing-transcripts problem (the important one). Let me answer the first and start digging into the second.

## Fixing the Supabase CLI token in this shell

`supabase login` stores the token in the **Windows Credential Manager**, which this non-interactive sandbox can't read. The robust fix (also the standard one for CI/automation) is to set the token as an **environment variable** instead, which any shell can see:

1. Create a Personal Access Token at https://supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens
2. Set it as a persistent **user** env var (new shells pick it up):
   ```powershell
   [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN','sbp_xxxxx','User')
   ```
   (or per-session: `$env:SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN='sbp_xxxxx'`)

`supabase` checks `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` before the keychain, so that bypasses the credential-store problem entirely. Want me to set it once you create the token?

## Now — why no transcripts are landing

That's the real issue. Let me investigate the ingestion path. First diagnostic: did yesterday's transcript land in **oracle.old** instead of current prod (a likely cutover artifact — the deployed worker may still write to the old DB), and have the Teams workers actually been running? Let me check both.

content.text:
This is decisive. The Jun 23 failure tells the story:

```
create failed (403): App '…' has reached its limit of '1'
'COMMUNICATIONS/ADHOCCALLS/GETALLTRANSCRIPTS' subscription on tenant '…'
```

And the successful runs are all `lifecycle:reauthorizationRequired` → renewing subscription `…`, expiry kept ~40 min out. So:

- The Graph **subscription exists and is being kept alive**, and the **webhook is reachable** (Graph is delivering *lifecycle* notifications to it — that's what's triggering these runs).
- But there are **zero transcript *change* notifications** being acted on (no ingestion runs in 14 weeks… 14 days; last actual transcript was Jun 4).
- The only subscription is on **`communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts`** — which per AGENTS.md captures **ad-hoc "meet now" calls only**, *not* scheduled meetings.

So the leading root cause: **your meeting yesterday was almost certainly a *scheduled* Teams meeting, and there is no subscription covering scheduled-meeting transcripts** — only the ad-hoc one. Let me confirm by checking whether the webhook received any transcript (non-lifecycle) notifications recently. Let me pull the Vercel logs for the webhook.
I need the Vercel team/project IDs. Let me check `.vercel/project.json`.

content.text:
The webhook itself is **healthy** — over the last 2 days it took 106 requests with **zero errors** (70× `200`, 36× `202`). Combined with everything else, the picture is now clear and consistent.

## Diagnosis: scheduled-meeting transcripts aren't subscribed at all

What the evidence shows:
- The webhook works and is receiving notifications — but they're **lifecycle** notifications (`reauthorizationRequired`), which is what keeps firing `teams-subscription-manager`. No errors, so nothing is silently failing.
- There is exactly **one** Graph subscription, and it's on **`communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts`** — confirmed by the `403 … reached its limit of '1' … ADHOCCALLS/GETALLTRANSCRIPTS` failure. Per AGENTS.md that resource is "the only capture path for **ad-hoc calls**."
- **Zero** `teams-transcript-ingestion` runs in 14 days; the last real transcript was **Jun 4** (in oracle.old).

So: **a normal *scheduled* Teams meeting (calendar invite) is not covered by any active subscription** — only ad-hoc "meet now" calls are. Scheduled-meeting capture would need a subscription on `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts`, which the production `teams-subscription-manager` does **not** create (there's only a `diagnose-transcripts.ps1` probe script for "scheduled meetings only" — it was never wired into the worker). That's why your recorded+transcribed meeting yesterday never landed.

(The two `teams-subscription-manager` failures are benign — it tried to *create* a second subscription when one already exists; it self-corrects on the next reauth. Not the cause. Also note: this is independent of today's migration/gate work — it's a pre-existing capture gap.)

One thing only you can confirm, because it determines the fix:

content.text: Now the webhook route and the subscription-manager task:

content.content:
1	// Increment 3 — Teams ad-hoc transcript subscription manager.
2	//
3	// Graph subscriptions for communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts max out
4	// near 1 hour and must be renewed by a machine, not a human (this is the
5	// "always-on listener stays armed" piece — no one re-authenticates anything).
6	//
7	// Two entry points, both calling the same idempotent ensureAdhocSubscription():
8	//   - teamsSubscriptionRenewCron: every 30 min, keeps the subscription alive.
9	//   - teamsSubscriptionManagerTask: triggered by the webhook on lifecycle events
10	//     (reauthorizationRequired / subscriptionRemoved / missed) for prompt repair.
11	//
12	// Requires the AZURE_* + TEAMS_* env in the Trigger.dev project (see
13	// ../lib/graph-transcripts.ts). When unset, ensureAdhocSubscription() no-ops
14	// with action 'skipped_no_config' rather than throwing.
15	
16	import { task, schedules } from '@trigger.dev/sdk/v3';
17	import { ensureAdhocSubscription, type EnsureResult } from '../lib/graph-transcripts';
18	
19	async function runEnsure(context: string): Promise<EnsureResult> {
20	  const result = await ensureAdhocSubscription();
21	  console.log(`[teams-subscription-manager] (${context})`, JSON.stringify(result));
22	  return result;
23	}
24	
25	// Webhook-triggered repair. Payload carries the lifecycle reason for logging.
26	export const teamsSubscriptionManagerTask = task({
27	  id: 'teams-subscription-manager',
28	  maxDuration: 60,
29	  run: async (payload: { reason?: string; subscriptionId?: string | null }) =>
30	    runEnsure(`lifecycle:${payload?.reason ?? 'manual'}`),
31	});
32	
33	// Periodic keep-alive.
34	export const teamsSubscriptionRenewCron = schedules.task({
35	  id: 'teams-subscription-renew',
36	  cron: '*/30 * * * *',
37	  maxDuration: 60,
38	  run: async () => runEnsure('cron'),
39	});
40

input.content:
# Plan: Capture scheduled-meeting Teams transcripts (going-forward + backfill)

## Context

Symptom: the Oracle isn't ingesting meeting transcripts. Albert had a recorded+transcribed **scheduled** Teams meeting on 2026-06-23 that never appeared.

Root cause (diagnosed): the only live Graph subscription is on `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts`, which captures **ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls only**. The webhook (`/api/teams/notifications`) is healthy (106 hits/2d, 0 errors; receives lifecycle `reauthorizationRequired` notifications) and `teams-subscription-manager` renews fine — but there is **no subscription for scheduled-meeting transcripts**, so `teams-transcript-ingestion` hasn't run in 14 days (last real transcript Jun 4). Scheduled online-meeting transcripts live under `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts`.

Goal: capture transcripts for **scheduled** Teams meetings going forward, **and** backfill recently-completed ones (incl. the Jun 23 meeting). Subscriptions only "listen going forward," so backfill needs a separate pull path.

Confirmed decisions: do both going-forward + backfill; Claude drives all code/worker actions and escalates to Albert only if Graph hard-blocks on a tenant-admin Teams/Entra policy (no Teams-admin creds in this env).

Builds on already-shipped work: ingested transcripts now land `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'` and surface in `/admin/transcripts` for approval (migrations `75`/`76` already applied to prod). The worker still needs redeploy to pick up that change — folded in here since both worker changes ship together.

## Part A — Going-forward: add the scheduled-meeting subscription

Key facts: the webhook is **resource-agnostic** (it decrypts any transcript notification and hands off to `teams-transcript-ingestion` — `apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts`), and the renew cron is **schedule-slot-neutral** if we reuse it (AGENTS.md §10: Trigger.dev is at 10/10 schedules — do NOT add a new `schedules.task()`).

1. **`apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts`** — generalize `ensureAdhocSubscription` (currently hardcodes `ADHOC_RESOURCE`, lines 108-172) into `ensureSubscription(resource: string)`; keep `ensureAdhocSubscription()` as a thin wrapper. Add `ONLINE_MEETINGS_RESOURCE = 'communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts'` and `ensureAllSubscriptions()` that ensures BOTH and returns combined results. Reuse `getToken`, `getSubscriptionConfigOrNull`, the list/find/PATCH/POST logic, and the same encryption cert (already works for adhoc). Harden the create path: treat a `403` "reached its limit of '1'" as "already exists" → re-list and renew instead of throwing (eliminates the benign manager failures seen Jun 22/23). Beta endpoint, matching adhoc; v1.0 fallback if beta 4xxs.
2. **`apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-subscription-manager.ts`** — `runEnsure` calls `ensureAllSubscriptions()`; the existing `teams-subscription-renew` cron (30 min) and the lifecycle-triggered `teams-subscription-manager` task now keep **both** subscriptions alive. No new schedule.
3. **`apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts`** — no functional change (already resource-agnostic); update the header comment to name both resources.
4. **`apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts`** (the "reference" copy AGENTS.md says to keep in sync) — add `ONLINE_MEETINGS_TRANSCRIPTS_RESOURCE` and parameterize `createAdhocTranscriptSubscription` (line 231) so both resources are representable; update the comment block (lines 160-175).

## Part B — Backfill: recover already-completed transcripts

5. **New worker `apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-backfill.ts`** — a `task` (triggered on-demand, **not** a cron → respects the 10/10 limit). Input `{ sinceDays?: number }` (default ~7). It:
   - Enumerates organizers (M365 users) — extend `graph-transcripts.ts` with a `listUsers()` returning `{ id }` (mirror of `listDisplayNameToEmail`'s paged `/users` fetch, lines 217-241).
   - For each organizer, `GET {beta}/communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='{id}',startDateTime=...,endDateTime=...)` over the window.
   - For each transcript returned, fire `triggerTask('teams-transcript-ingestion', { transcriptContentUrl, resourcePath, meetingId, callId })` — same handoff shape the webhook uses. Idempotent: the ingestion worker already dedupes on `clientMessageId LIKE 'teams:<transcriptId>:%'` (teams-transcript-ingestion.ts:136-148), so re-runs are safe.
   - Reuses `getToken`, `fetchTranscriptVtt` (already handles a transcript content URL/resource path), and the gated ingestion worker (lands `awaiting_approval`).
6. **Run it once** via the Trigger MCP (`trigger_task` `teams-transcript-backfill` `{sinceDays: 7}`, env prod) after deploy to recover the Jun 23 meeting.

## Permissions / ops (Claude drives; escalate only if hard-blocked)
- App permission `OnlineMeetingTranscript.Read.All` is already granted (AGENTS.md §7), so the subscription create + `getAllTranscripts` pull should work with existing app-only creds.
- The subscription create runs in the worker via the app secret — no tenant-admin action by itself. The only thing Claude can't do here is change a **Teams Cloud-Communications application access policy** (needs Teams-admin auth). If — and only if — a create/pull returns a `403` *authorization/policy* error (distinct from the benign per-resource "limit of 1"), stop and hand Albert the exact `New-CsApplicationAccessPolicy` / `Grant-CsApplicationAccessPolicy` commands, then resume.

## Docs
- AGENTS.md §7 (transcript-subscription row → both resources) + §6 (ingestion row) + the §10 "schedule slots full" note (reused cron, no new schedule); `docs/architecture.md` "Teams transcript ingestion"; a DECISIONS.md entry for adding the scheduled-meeting path.

## Out of scope
- The live Recall path (unchanged). The deterministic candidate-before-claim pipeline (unchanged). No new Trigger.dev schedule.

## Verification
1. **Types/build:** `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run typecheck`; `--filter @oracle/web run typecheck`.
2. **Deploy worker:** `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (also ships the already-written `awaiting_approval` ingestion change).
3. **Subscriptions live:** via Trigger MCP, trigger `teams-subscription-renew`/manager and confirm logs/`list subscriptions` show BOTH `adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts` and `onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts` pointing at our `notificationUrl` with a future expiry.
4. **Backfill:** `trigger_task teams-transcript-backfill {sinceDays:7}` (prod); confirm a `teams-transcript-ingestion` run fires, a `raw_transcripts` row appears (`approval_status='pending_approval'`, messages `awaiting_approval`), and the Jun 23 meeting shows in `/admin/transcripts`. (DB check via the verified prod connection string, read-only.)
5. **Going-forward:** hold a scheduled test meeting with transcription on; confirm ingestion fires within minutes and it appears in `/admin/transcripts` for approval.
6. If any Graph call 403s on a policy: escalate per "Permissions / ops" above.

lines: -# Plan: Claims grouping, shift-click selection, and a transcript approval gate
+# Plan: Capture scheduled-meeting Teams transcripts (going-forward + backfill)
-Three related asks in the admin **Claims** area (`/admin/claims`) and the meeting-transcript pipeline:
+Symptom: the Oracle isn't ingesting meeting transcripts. Albert had a recorded+transcribed **scheduled** Teams meeting on 2026-06-23 that never appeared.

-1. **Group claims by source** — the claims table is currently one flat list sorted by `created_at DESC`. Albert wants claims grouped by their *specific* origin: each meeting (channel) or each document is its own group.

-2. **Shift-click range selection** — the row checkboxes are plain HTML inputs wired to forms via `form="bulk-evaluate"` / `form="translate-claims"` with no JS. Albert wants to shift-click to select a contiguous range.

-3. **Approve a meeting transcript before it is ingested** — today there is **no approval step**. When the Oracle is brought into a Teams meeting, `teams-transcript-ingestion` fires from the Graph webhook and writes utterances straight into `messages` as `extraction_status='pending'`; the extraction cron then auto-extracts claims. Albert wants a screen to review each meeting transcript and approve (or reject) it *before* claims are extracted.

+Root cause (diagnosed): the only live Graph subscription is on `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts`, which captures **ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls only**. The webhook (`/api/teams/notifications`) is healthy (106 hits/2d, 0 errors; receives lifecycle `reauthorizationRequired` notifications) and `teams-subscription-manager` renews fine — but there is **no subscription for scheduled-meeting transcripts**, so `teams-transcript-ingestion` hasn't run in 14 days (last real transcript Jun 4). Scheduled online-meeting transcripts live under `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts`.
-- Grouping granularity: **by specific meeting/document** (one group header per channel or document).
-- Gate scope: **Graph ad-hoc transcripts only** (`teams-transcript-ingestion`). The live Recall path stays real-time as today.
-- On reject: **keep the messages, mark them `skipped`** (reversible; raw VTT always retained in `raw_transcripts`).

+Goal: capture transcripts for **scheduled** Teams meetings going forward, **and** backfill recently-completed ones (incl. the Jun 23 meeting). Subscriptions only "listen going forward," so backfill needs a separate pull path.

+Confirmed decisions: do both going-forward + backfill; Claude drives all code/worker actions and escalates to Albert only if Graph hard-blocks on a tenant-admin Teams/Entra policy (no Teams-admin creds in this env).
-All three claim-extraction workers select work with exactly `extraction_status = 'pending' AND role = 'user'`

-(`claim-extraction.ts:207`, `claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts:146`). So the cleanest, lowest-blast-radius gate is a **new terminal-until-approved extraction status**: ingest held transcripts as `extraction_status = 'awaiting_approval'`. The cron never sees them. Approval flips the channel's held messages to `'pending'` (they flow normally); rejection flips them to `'skipped'`. **No change to any extraction worker query.**

+Builds on already-shipped work: ingested transcripts now land `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'` and surface in `/admin/transcripts` for approval (migrations `75`/`76` already applied to prod). The worker still needs redeploy to pick up that change — folded in here since both worker changes ship together.
-## Part A — Claims grouped by meeting/document
+## Part A — Going-forward: add the scheduled-meeting subscription

+Key facts: the webhook is **resource-agnostic** (it decrypts any transcript notification and hands off to `teams-transcript-ingestion` — `apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts`), and the renew cron is **schedule-slot-neutral** if we reuse it (AGENTS.md §10: Trigger.dev is at 10/10 schedules — do NOT add a new `schedules.task()`).

-1. Extend the existing `LATERAL` evidence subquery (currently picks the highest-confidence `claim_evidence` row, `page.tsx:108-114`) to also return a stable `source_group_key` and `source_group_label`, derived from the evidence's source:

-   - `source_message_id` → `messages.channel_id` → `channels.name` (meeting/channel). Key = `chan:<channel_id>`, label = channel name (Teams calls are named `Teams call … UTC`). Use `messages.metadata_json->>'source'` only to pick an icon (🎙 transcript vs 💬 chat).
-   - else (`manual_admin` / no evidence) → key = `manual`, label = "Manual / no source".
-2. In the page, group `rows` by `source_group_key` (preserving each group's most-recent-first order; order groups by their newest claim's `created_at DESC`).

-3. Render a **group header row** (`<tr>` spanning all columns) before each group: icon + label + claim count. Keep the existing per-row markup unchanged otherwise. Grouping is applied within the active status tab (tabs unchanged).

+1. **`apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts`** — generalize `ensureAdhocSubscription` (currently hardcodes `ADHOC_RESOURCE`, lines 108-172) into `ensureSubscription(resource: string)`; keep `ensureAdhocSubscription()` as a thin wrapper. Beta endpoint, matching adhoc; v1.0 fallback if beta 4xxs.

+2. **`apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-subscription-manager.ts`** — `runEnsure` calls `ensureAllSubscriptions()`; the existing `teams-subscription-renew` cron (30 min) and the lifecycle-triggered `teams-subscription-manager` task now keep **both** subscriptions alive. No new schedule.

+4. **`apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts`** (the "reference" copy AGENTS.md says to keep in sync) — add `ONLINE_MEETINGS_TRANSCRIPTS_RESOURCE` and parameterize `createAdhocTranscriptSubscription` (line 231) so both resources are representable; update the comment block (lines 160-175).
+## Part B — Backfill: recover already-completed transcripts
-Keep checkboxes server-rendered and form-associated (submission unchanged). Add a tiny client "enhancer".

+5. **New worker `apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-backfill.ts`** — a `task` (triggered on-demand, **not** a cron → respects the 10/10 limit). Input `{ sinceDays?: number }` (default ~7). It:
+   - Enumerates organizers (M365 users) — extend `graph-transcripts.ts` with a `listUsers()` returning `{ id }` (mirror of `listDisplayNameToEmail`'s paged `/users` fetch, lines 217-241).

Pure DOM (`.checked =`), no React state, ranges never cross form groups (so pending vs approved selections stay independent even within one meeting group).
+## Permissions / ops (Claude drives; escalate only if hard-blocked)
+- App permission `OnlineMeetingTranscript.Read.All` is already granted (AGENTS.md §7), so the subscription create + `getAllTranscripts` pull should work with existing app-only creds.

The only thing Claude can't do here is change a **Teams Cloud-Communications application access policy** (needs Teams-admin auth). +6. If any Graph call 403s on a policy: escalate per "Permissions / ops" above.

# Plan: Claims grouping, shift-click selection, and a transcript approval gate

## Context

Three related asks in the admin **Claims** area (`/admin/claims`) and the meeting-transcript pipeline:

1. **Group claims by source** — the claims table is currently one flat list sorted by `created_at DESC`. Albert wants claims grouped by their *specific* origin: each meeting (channel) or each document is its own group.
2. **Shift-click range selection** — the row checkboxes are plain HTML inputs wired to forms via `form="bulk-evaluate"` / `form="translate-claims"` with no JS. Albert wants to shift-click to select a contiguous range.
3. **Approve a meeting transcript before it is ingested** — today there is **no approval step**. When the Oracle is brought into a Teams meeting, `teams-transcript-ingestion` fires from the Graph webhook and writes utterances straight into `messages` as `extraction_status='pending'`; the extraction cron then auto-extracts claims. Albert wants a screen to review each meeting transcript and approve (or reject) it *before* claims are extracted.

Confirmed decisions:
- Grouping granularity: **by specific meeting/document** (one group header per channel or document).
- Gate scope: **Graph ad-hoc transcripts only** (`teams-transcript-ingestion`). The live Recall path stays real-time as today.
- On reject: **keep the messages, mark them `skipped`** (reversible; raw VTT always retained in `raw_transcripts`).

## Architecture of the gate (key idea)

All three claim-extraction workers select work with exactly `extraction_status = 'pending' AND role = 'user'`
(`claim-extraction.ts:207`, `claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts:146`). So the cleanest, lowest-blast-radius gate is a **new terminal-until-approved extraction status**: ingest held transcripts as `extraction_status = 'awaiting_approval'`. The cron never sees them. Approval flips the channel's held messages to `'pending'` (they flow normally); rejection flips them to `'skipped'`. **No change to any extraction worker query.**

## Part A — Claims grouped by meeting/document

**File:** `apps/web/app/admin/claims/page.tsx` (server component; pure SQL + render change).

1. Extend the existing `LATERAL` evidence subquery (currently picks the highest-confidence `claim_evidence` row, `page.tsx:108-114`) to also return a stable `source_group_key` and `source_group_label`, derived from the evidence's source:
   - `source_message_id` → `messages.channel_id` → `channels.name` (meeting/channel). Key = `chan:<channel_id>`, label = channel name (Teams calls are named `Teams call … UTC`). Use `messages.metadata_json->>'source'` only to pick an icon (🎙 transcript vs 💬 chat).
   - `source_document_chunk_id` → `document_chunks.document_id` → `documents.file_name`. Key = `doc:<document_id>`, label = file name.
   - `source_external_record_id` → key = `ext:<value>`, label = "External: <value>".
   - else (`manual_admin` / no evidence) → key = `manual`, label = "Manual / no source".
   Join columns confirmed: `claim_evidence.source_message_id/source_document_chunk_id/source_external_record_id` (`schema.ts:649-653`), `documents.file_name` (`schema.ts:429`), `channels.name` (`schema.ts:343`).
2. In the page, group `rows` by `source_group_key` (preserving each group's most-recent-first order; order groups by their newest claim's `created_at DESC`).
3. Render a **group header row** (`<tr>` spanning all columns) before each group: icon + label + claim count. Keep the existing per-row markup unchanged otherwise. Grouping is applied within the active status tab (tabs unchanged).

## Part B — Shift-click range selection

Keep checkboxes server-rendered and form-associated (submission unchanged). Add a tiny client "enhancer".

1. In `page.tsx`, add data attributes to each checkbox: `data-claim-select`, `data-select-form={"translate-claims"|"bulk-evaluate"}`, and `data-select-index` = the checkbox's index **within its own form group** (compute two running counters while mapping rows).
2. New client component `apps/web/app/admin/claims/_components/shift-select.tsx` (`'use client'`), rendered once inside the Card. On mount it attaches one delegated `click` listener: when a `[data-claim-select]` checkbox is shift-clicked, it toggles every checkbox **sharing the same `data-select-form`** between the last-clicked index and the current index to the current checked state. Pure DOM (`.checked =`), no React state, ranges never cross form groups (so pending vs approved selections stay independent even within one meeting group).

## Part C — Transcript approval gate

### C1. Migrations
- **Enum value (Drizzle-generated):** add `'awaiting_approval'` to `extractionStatusEnum` in `packages/db/src/schema.ts:79`, then `pnpm db:generate` to emit the `ALTER TYPE … ADD VALUE` migration under `packages/db/migrations/0*.sql`. (Generated migrations are journal-tracked → run once.)
- **`raw_transcripts` approval columns (hand-written idempotent):** new `packages/db/migrations/sql/75_transcript_approval.sql`. `raw_transcripts` is not in the Drizzle schema (`62_raw_transcripts.sql`), so this stays hand-written. Wrap in a single `DO $$ … IF NOT EXISTS(column) …` block so the one-time backfill runs exactly once on re-boot:
  - add `approval_status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending_approval'` (`pending_approval`|`approved`|`rejected`), `reviewed_by_employee_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id)`, `reviewed_at timestamptz`, `review_note text`.
  - backfill `UPDATE raw_transcripts SET approval_status='approved'` inside the same guard — transcripts ingested before the gate already flowed to extraction, so they must not retroactively appear "pending".

### C2. Ingestion worker
**File:** `apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-ingestion.ts:269`. Change the inserted message `extractionStatus` from `'pending'` to `'awaiting_approval'`. The `raw_transcripts` insert (`:253-257`) already lands with default `approval_status='pending_approval'`. Nothing else changes (the worker still does speaker resolution, channel creation, raw-VTT persistence). Requires a worker redeploy (`corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy`).

### C3. New admin page `/admin/transcripts`
**Files:** `apps/web/app/admin/transcripts/page.tsx` + `_actions.ts` (model after `apps/web/app/admin/messages/page.tsx` and the claims `_actions.ts` admin-guard pattern).
- Page (server component): list `raw_transcripts` joined to `channels`, default-filtered to `approval_status='pending_approval'` (tabs for approved/rejected/all). Per transcript show: meeting/channel name + time, message/utterance count, resolved speaker names, and an expandable preview of utterances (from `messages`, ordered by `created_at`). Approve / Reject buttons (Reject has an optional note).
- `_actions.ts` server actions, **admin-only guard** (reuse the same auth check the claims `_actions.ts` uses):
  - `approveTranscript(channelId)`: set `raw_transcripts.approval_status='approved'`, `reviewed_by_employee_id`, `reviewed_at=now()`; flip that channel's `messages` from `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'` → `'pending'`. Then the existing extraction cron picks them up.
  - `rejectTranscript(channelId, note)`: set `approval_status='rejected'` + reviewer fields; flip channel's `awaiting_approval` messages → `'skipped'`. Reversible (re-approve flips skipped→pending for that channel).
  - `revalidatePath('/admin/transcripts')` after each.

### C4. Nav
**File:** `apps/web/app/admin/_components/admin-nav.tsx:39-43`. Add `{ href: '/admin/transcripts', label: 'Transcripts' }` to the **Activity** group, next to Messages.

## Out of scope / notes
- Live Recall utterance path (`teams-live-recall-utterance.ts`) is intentionally **not** gated.
- No changes to extraction worker queries, the candidate-before-claim validators, or claim review.
- AGENTS.md "Task-to-file" rows for Teams ingestion and admin pages will be updated as part of the work.

## Verification
1. **Build/types:** `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/web run build` (or `lint`/`typecheck`) — confirms the new enum value, page, and actions typecheck.
2. **Grouping + shift-click (preview tools):** start the web app, open `/admin/claims`. Confirm group headers per meeting/document and counts; shift-click two checkboxes in one group and confirm the in-between boxes toggle, and that a range never crosses the pending/approved form boundary. Screenshot.
3. **Gate (DB-level, safe to test locally):** after migration, insert a fake `raw_transcripts`+channel+`awaiting_approval` messages (or trigger `teams-transcript-ingestion` in dev); confirm the extraction cron does **not** pick them up while held. Hit Approve on `/admin/transcripts`, confirm messages flip to `pending` and the next extraction run consumes them; hit Reject on another, confirm `skipped` and no extraction.
4. **Backfill check:** confirm pre-existing `raw_transcripts` rows show `approved` (not `pending_approval`) so historical meetings don't flood the new queue.
5. **Migrations:** run via `pnpm db:migrate` only (never Supabase MCP / drizzle-kit push). Do not apply to prod without Albert's go-ahead.

# Plan: Capture scheduled-meeting Teams transcripts (going-forward + backfill)

## Context

Symptom: the Oracle isn't ingesting meeting transcripts. Albert had a recorded+transcribed **scheduled** Teams meeting on 2026-06-23 that never appeared.

Root cause (diagnosed): the only live Graph subscription is on `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts`, which captures **ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls only**. The webhook (`/api/teams/notifications`) is healthy (106 hits/2d, 0 errors; receives lifecycle `reauthorizationRequired` notifications) and `teams-subscription-manager` renews fine — but there is **no subscription for scheduled-meeting transcripts**, so `teams-transcript-ingestion` hasn't run in 14 days (last real transcript Jun 4). Scheduled online-meeting transcripts live under `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts`.

Goal: capture transcripts for **scheduled** Teams meetings going forward, **and** backfill recently-completed ones (incl. the Jun 23 meeting). Subscriptions only "listen going forward," so backfill needs a separate pull path.

Confirmed decisions: do both going-forward + backfill; Claude drives all code/worker actions and escalates to Albert only if Graph hard-blocks on a tenant-admin Teams/Entra policy (no Teams-admin creds in this env).

Builds on already-shipped work: ingested transcripts now land `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'` and surface in `/admin/transcripts` for approval (migrations `75`/`76` already applied to prod). The worker still needs redeploy to pick up that change — folded in here since both worker changes ship together.

## Part A — Going-forward: add the scheduled-meeting subscription

Key facts: the webhook is **resource-agnostic** (it decrypts any transcript notification and hands off to `teams-transcript-ingestion` — `apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts`), and the renew cron is **schedule-slot-neutral** if we reuse it (AGENTS.md §10: Trigger.dev is at 10/10 schedules — do NOT add a new `schedules.task()`).

1. **`apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts`** — generalize `ensureAdhocSubscription` (currently hardcodes `ADHOC_RESOURCE`, lines 108-172) into `ensureSubscription(resource: string)`; keep `ensureAdhocSubscription()` as a thin wrapper. Add `ONLINE_MEETINGS_RESOURCE = 'communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts'` and `ensureAllSubscriptions()` that ensures BOTH and returns combined results. Reuse `getToken`, `getSubscriptionConfigOrNull`, the list/find/PATCH/POST logic, and the same encryption cert (already works for adhoc). Harden the create path: treat a `403` "reached its limit of '1'" as "already exists" → re-list and renew instead of throwing (eliminates the benign manager failures seen Jun 22/23). Beta endpoint, matching adhoc; v1.0 fallback if beta 4xxs.
2. **`apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-subscription-manager.ts`** — `runEnsure` calls `ensureAllSubscriptions()`; the existing `teams-subscription-renew` cron (30 min) and the lifecycle-triggered `teams-subscription-manager` task now keep **both** subscriptions alive. No new schedule.
3. **`apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts`** — no functional change (already resource-agnostic); update the header comment to name both resources.
4. **`apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts`** (the "reference" copy AGENTS.md says to keep in sync) — add `ONLINE_MEETINGS_TRANSCRIPTS_RESOURCE` and parameterize `createAdhocTranscriptSubscription` (line 231) so both resources are representable; update the comment block (lines 160-175).

## Part B — Backfill: recover already-completed transcripts

5. **New worker `apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-backfill.ts`** — a `task` (triggered on-demand, **not** a cron → respects the 10/10 limit). Input `{ sinceDays?: number }` (default ~7). It:
   - Enumerates organizers (M365 users) — extend `graph-transcripts.ts` with a `listUsers()` returning `{ id }` (mirror of `listDisplayNameToEmail`'s paged `/users` fetch, lines 217-241).
   - For each organizer, `GET {beta}/communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='{id}',startDateTime=...,endDateTime=...)` over the window.
   - For each transcript returned, fire `triggerTask('teams-transcript-ingestion', { transcriptContentUrl, resourcePath, meetingId, callId })` — same handoff shape the webhook uses. Idempotent: the ingestion worker already dedupes on `clientMessageId LIKE 'teams:<transcriptId>:%'` (teams-transcript-ingestion.ts:136-148), so re-runs are safe.
   - Reuses `getToken`, `fetchTranscriptVtt` (already handles a transcript content URL/resource path), and the gated ingestion worker (lands `awaiting_approval`).
6. **Run it once** via the Trigger MCP (`trigger_task` `teams-transcript-backfill` `{sinceDays: 7}`, env prod) after deploy to recover the Jun 23 meeting.

## Permissions / ops (Claude drives; escalate only if hard-blocked)
- App permission `OnlineMeetingTranscript.Read.All` is already granted (AGENTS.md §7), so the subscription create + `getAllTranscripts` pull should work with existing app-only creds.
- The subscription create runs in the worker via the app secret — no tenant-admin action by itself. The only thing Claude can't do here is change a **Teams Cloud-Communications application access policy** (needs Teams-admin auth). If — and only if — a create/pull returns a `403` *authorization/policy* error (distinct from the benign per-resource "limit of 1"), stop and hand Albert the exact `New-CsApplicationAccessPolicy` / `Grant-CsApplicationAccessPolicy` commands, then resume.

## Docs
- AGENTS.md §7 (transcript-subscription row → both resources) + §6 (ingestion row) + the §10 "schedule slots full" note (reused cron, no new schedule); `docs/architecture.md` "Teams transcript ingestion"; a DECISIONS.md entry for adding the scheduled-meeting path.

## Out of scope
- The live Recall path (unchanged). The deterministic candidate-before-claim pipeline (unchanged). No new Trigger.dev schedule.

## Verification
1. **Types/build:** `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run typecheck`; `--filter @oracle/web run typecheck`.
2. **Deploy worker:** `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (also ships the already-written `awaiting_approval` ingestion change).
3. **Subscriptions live:** via Trigger MCP, trigger `teams-subscription-renew`/manager and confirm logs/`list subscriptions` show BOTH `adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts` and `onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts` pointing at our `notificationUrl` with a future expiry.
4. **Backfill:** `trigger_task teams-transcript-backfill {sinceDays:7}` (prod); confirm a `teams-transcript-ingestion` run fires, a `raw_transcripts` row appears (`approval_status='pending_approval'`, messages `awaiting_approval`), and the Jun 23 meeting shows in `/admin/transcripts`. (DB check via the verified prod connection string, read-only.)
5. **Going-forward:** hold a scheduled test meeting with transcription on; confirm ingestion fires within minutes and it appears in `/admin/transcripts` for approval.
6. If any Graph call 403s on a policy: escalate per "Permissions / ops" above.

5. **New worker `apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-backfill.ts`** — a `task` (triggered on-demand, **not** a cron → respects the 10/10 limit). Input `{ sinceDays?: number }` (default ~7). It:
   - Enumerates organizers (M365 users) — extend `graph-transcripts.ts` with a `listUsers()` returning `{ id }` (mirror of `listDisplayNameToEmail`'s paged `/users` fetch, lines 217-241).
   - For each organizer, `GET {beta}/communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='{id}',startDateTime=...,endDateTime=...)` over the window.
   - For each transcript returned, fire `triggerTask('teams-transcript-ingestion', { transcriptContentUrl, resourcePath, meetingId, callId })` — same handoff shape the webhook uses. Idempotent: the ingestion worker already dedupes on `clientMessageId LIKE 'teams:<transcriptId>:%'` (teams-transcript-ingestion.ts:136-148), so re-runs are safe.
   - Reuses `getToken`, `fetchTranscriptVtt` (already handles a transcript content URL/resource path), and the gated ingestion worker (lands `awaiting_approval`).
6. **Run it once** via the Trigger MCP (`trigger_task` `teams-transcript-backfill` `{sinceDays: 7}`, env prod) after deploy to recover the Jun 23 meeting.

## Permissions / ops (Claude drives; escalate only if hard-blocked)
- App permission `OnlineMeetingTranscript.Read.All` is already granted (AGENTS.md §7), so the subscription create + `getAllTranscripts` pull should work with existing app-only creds.
- The subscription create runs in the worker via the app secret — no tenant-admin action by itself. The only thing Claude can't do here is change a **Teams Cloud-Communications application access policy** (needs Teams-admin auth). If — and only if — a create/pull returns a `403` *authorization/policy* error (distinct from the benign per-resource "limit of 1"), stop and hand Albert the exact `New-CsApplicationAccessPolicy` / `Grant-CsApplicationAccessPolicy` commands, then resume.

## Out of scope
- The live Recall path (unchanged). The deterministic candidate-before-claim pipeline (unchanged). No new Trigger.dev schedule.

# Plan: Capture scheduled-meeting Teams transcripts (going-forward + backfill)

## Context

Symptom: the Oracle isn't ingesting meeting transcripts. Albert had a recorded+transcribed **scheduled** Teams meeting on 2026-06-23 that never appeared.

Root cause (diagnosed): the only live Graph subscription is on `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts`, which captures **ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls only**. The webhook (`/api/teams/notifications`) is healthy (106 hits/2d, 0 errors; receives lifecycle `reauthorizationRequired` notifications) and `teams-subscription-manager` renews fine — but there is **no subscription for scheduled-meeting transcripts**, so `teams-transcript-ingestion` hasn't run in 14 days (last real transcript Jun 4). Scheduled online-meeting transcripts live under `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts`.

Goal: capture transcripts for **scheduled** Teams meetings going forward, **and** backfill recently-completed ones (incl. the Jun 23 meeting). Subscriptions only "listen going forward," so backfill needs a separate pull path.

Confirmed decisions: do both going-forward + backfill; Claude drives all code/worker actions and escalates to Albert only if Graph hard-blocks on a tenant-admin Teams/Entra policy (no Teams-admin creds in this env).

Builds on already-shipped work: ingested transcripts now land `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'` and surface in `/admin/transcripts` for approval (migrations `75`/`76` already applied to prod). The worker still needs redeploy to pick up that change — folded in here since both worker changes ship together.

## Part A — Going-forward: add the scheduled-meeting subscription

Key facts: the webhook is **resource-agnostic** (it decrypts any transcript notification and hands off to `teams-transcript-ingestion` — `apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts`), and the renew cron is **schedule-slot-neutral** if we reuse it (AGENTS.md §10: Trigger.dev is at 10/10 schedules — do NOT add a new `schedules.task()`).

1. **`apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts`** — generalize `ensureAdhocSubscription` (currently hardcodes `ADHOC_RESOURCE`, lines 108-172) into `ensureSubscription(resource: string)`; keep `ensureAdhocSubscription()` as a thin wrapper. Reuse `getToken`, `getSubscriptionConfigOrNull`, the list/find/PATCH/POST logic, and the same encryption cert (already works for adhoc). 500	## D-transcript-fuzzy-quote — fuzzy quote match for spoken transcripts (2026-06-04)
501	
502	- **Decision**: `validateQuote` gains an opt-in `allowFuzzy` path. After strict verbatim + normalized matching fail, if the model quote's content tokens overlap the source utterance by >= `fuzzyMinOverlap` (default 0.5), accept as `normalized_match` (method `fuzzy_token_overlap`) and anchor the stored evidence to the REAL source utterance text, not the model's paraphrase. Enabled only on the message/transcript path (`claim-extraction.ts`); documents stay strict.
503	- **Why**: First real conversation to hit extraction was a Teams call. Spoken transcripts are disfluent and the model paraphrases them, so the polished quote never appears verbatim — strict matching rejected ~every transcript claim. Product-owner call (2026-06-04) to loosen for spoken sources.
504	- **Auditability**: deterministic token check (no LLM grader), and evidence stays a real transcript span — only the "model must reproduce verbatim" requirement is relaxed. Partially overrides the "no fuzzy" note in quote-validator.ts / docs/oracle/03. Knobs: fuzzyMinOverlap (0.5), >=4-token floor.

507

509	- **Why**: `messages` are a lossy transform (merged turns, resolved speakers, dropped timing) and Microsoft expires ad-hoc transcripts. Raw VTT keeps the whole pipeline re-runnable from true source for iterative fine-tuning.

file.content:
## D-transcript-fuzzy-quote — fuzzy quote match for spoken transcripts (2026-06-04)

- **Decision**: `validateQuote` gains an opt-in `allowFuzzy` path. After strict verbatim + normalized matching fail, if the model quote's content tokens overlap the source utterance by >= `fuzzyMinOverlap` (default 0.5), accept as `normalized_match` (method `fuzzy_token_overlap`) and anchor the stored evidence to the REAL source utterance text, not the model's paraphrase. Enabled only on the message/transcript path (`claim-extraction.ts`); documents stay strict.
- **Why**: First real conversation to hit extraction was a Teams call. Spoken transcripts are disfluent and the model paraphrases them, so the polished quote never appears verbatim — strict matching rejected ~every transcript claim. Product-owner call (2026-06-04) to loosen for spoken sources.
- **Auditability**: deterministic token check (no LLM grader), and evidence stays a real transcript span — only the "model must reproduce verbatim" requirement is relaxed. Partially overrides the "no fuzzy" note in quote-validator.ts / docs/oracle/03. Knobs: fuzzyMinOverlap (0.5), >=4-token floor.

## D-raw-transcripts — persist raw VTT for re-runnability (2026-06-04)

- **Decision**: Persist each call's raw WebVTT in `raw_transcripts` at ingestion (idempotent on transcript_id). Hand-written `migrations/sql/62_raw_transcripts.sql`; worker uses raw `sql`, not in schema.ts.
- **Why**: `messages` are a lossy transform (merged turns, resolved speakers, dropped timing) and Microsoft expires ad-hoc transcripts. Raw VTT keeps the whole pipeline re-runnable from true source for iterative fine-tuning.

# Document ingestion: Word, image vision, auxiliary models, context (2026-06-14)

input.old_string:
## D-raw-transcripts — persist raw VTT for re-runnability (2026-06-04)

- **Decision**: Persist each call's raw WebVTT in `raw_transcripts` at ingestion (idempotent on transcript_id). Hand-written `migrations/sql/62_raw_transcripts.sql`; worker uses raw `sql`, not in schema.ts.
- **Why**: `messages` are a lossy transform (merged turns, resolved speakers, dropped timing) and Microsoft expires ad-hoc transcripts. Raw VTT keeps the whole pipeline re-runnable from true source for iterative fine-tuning.
input.new_string:
## D-raw-transcripts — persist raw VTT for re-runnability (2026-06-04)

- **Decision**: Persist each call's raw WebVTT in `raw_transcripts` at ingestion (idempotent on transcript_id). Hand-written `migrations/sql/62_raw_transcripts.sql`; worker uses raw `sql`, not in schema.ts.
- **Why**: `messages` are a lossy transform (merged turns, resolved speakers, dropped timing) and Microsoft expires ad-hoc transcripts. Raw VTT keeps the whole pipeline re-runnable from true source for iterative fine-tuning.

## D-transcript-approval-gate — human approval before transcript extraction (2026-06-24)

- **Decision**: Teams transcripts are ingested as `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'` (new enum value; `migrations/sql/75`) and held until an admin approves them at `/admin/transcripts`. `raw_transcripts.approval_status` (`migrations/sql/76`) tracks the decision; approve flips the channel's messages → `pending`, reject → `skipped`. The extraction cron is unchanged (it only ever selects `pending`).
- **Why**: Albert wanted to review meeting transcripts before they produce claims, rather than the prior auto-ingest-then-review-at-claim-level flow. Lowest-blast-radius gate — no extraction-worker change.

## D-scheduled-meeting-transcripts — capture scheduled meetings, not just ad-hoc (2026-06-24)

- **Decision**: Add a second standing subscription on `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts` alongside the ad-hoc one (`ensureAllSubscriptions` keeps both alive via the existing `teams-subscription-renew` cron — no new schedule, respecting the 10/10 limit). Add an on-demand `teams-transcript-backfill` task to recover already-completed scheduled transcripts (subscriptions only listen going forward).
- **Why**: Only ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls were being captured; normal scheduled meetings (the common case) produced nothing — last real transcript was 2026-06-04. The webhook is resource-agnostic, so this is a subscription + backfill change with no webhook/ingestion rework.

content.content:
232	## Teams transcript ingestion (Microsoft Graph)
233	
234	Pulls Microsoft Teams **call transcripts** into the same evidence pipeline as chat and documents. POP's meetings are all ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls, never scheduled — which constrains the design (see below). Status: **LIVE + validated end-to-end** for post-call transcript ingestion (2026-06-04/05).
235	
236	```
237	Teams call (transcription ON)
238	  ↓  Microsoft publishes a transcript after the call ends
239	Microsoft Graph change-notification subscription
240	  resource: communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts  (BETA only; v1.0 rejects it)
241	  ↓  POST (encrypted) to notificationUrl
242	apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts   (the "always-on listener", on Vercel)
243	  • validation handshake (echo ?validationToken — Graph checks this at create time)
244	  • verify clientState, decrypt the rich payload (graph-notification-crypto.ts:
245	    RSA-OAEP unwrap → HMAC-SHA256 verify → AES-256-CBC decrypt)
246	  • triggerTask('teams-transcript-ingestion', { transcriptContentUrl, meetingId, ... })
247	  ↓
248	apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-ingestion.ts
249	  • fetch the WebVTT (graph-transcripts.ts:fetchTranscriptVtt)
250	  • parseVtt → mergeBySpeaker → one utterance per turn
251	  • resolve speaker → employee by display-name (null when unmatched)
252	  • INSERT a channel (per call) + channel_participants + messages
253	    (role='user', extraction_status='pending', clientMessageId='teams:<transcriptId>:<n>')
254	  ↓
255	existing claim-extraction cron picks up the pending messages  →  candidate-before-claim
256	```
257	
258	Each utterance becomes a `messages` row (not a `document_chunk`) precisely so it carries speaker attribution (`employeeId`) and verbatim quotes — the same evidence shape as chat. Idempotent: re-running for the same transcript is a no-op (the `clientMessageId` dedupe key). Nothing here writes to `claims` — the normal R5/R6 validators run downstream.
259	
260	Subscription lifecycle is owned by `teams-subscription-manager.ts`: the `teams-subscription-renew` cron (`*/30`) and a webhook-lifecycle-triggered task both call the idempotent `ensureAdhocSubscription()` (renew if <20 min left, else create). The resource max-lifetime is ~1h, so a machine must keep re-upping it — no human re-authenticates anything.
261	
262	**Hard constraints (derived from Microsoft Graph, not choices):**
263	- **Ad-hoc calls are only reachable via a subscription, and only on beta.** `getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId=...)` (used by `diagnose-transcripts.ps1`) enumerates *scheduled* meetings only; ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls never appear there. The subscription is "listen going forward" — a transcript only notifies if the subscription existed *before* transcription started; past calls are unrecoverable.
264	- **No Graph live transcript / no Graph live spoken awareness.** Graph exposes no API to read a meeting's live caption/transcript stream, and Teams does not pipe spoken words into the meeting text chat. The Graph path therefore ingests calls *after* they end. Live spoken participation is a separate Recall.ai meeting-bot path, not a Graph capability.
265	- **The webhook must be deployed before the subscription can be created** — Graph validates `notificationUrl` synchronously at create time.
266	
267	The Graph subscription/transcript surface is intentionally duplicated: `apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts` (web/webhook side) and `apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts` (worker side), because apps/web and apps/workers are separate processes and cross-app imports aren't allowed. The web copy is the reference. See AGENTS.md §10.
268	
269	**Status: LIVE + validated end-to-end (2026-06-04/05).** Real call → 95 speaker-attributed messages → claims. Refinements made while validating on real data:
270	
271	- **Speaker resolution is email-based.** VTT display name → M365 directory email (Graph `/users`) → employee by `employees.email` or `employee_identities.email`; an unmatched `@popcre.com` speaker bootstraps a provisional employee. (`employees.name` often differs from the transcript display name, so name-matching alone failed.)

## Teams transcript ingestion (Microsoft Graph)

Pulls Microsoft Teams **call transcripts** into the same evidence pipeline as chat and documents. POP's meetings are all ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls, never scheduled — which constrains the design (see below). Status: **LIVE + validated end-to-end** for post-call transcript ingestion (2026-06-04/05).

```
Teams call (transcription ON)
  ↓  Microsoft publishes a transcript after the call ends
Microsoft Graph change-notification subscription
  resource: communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts  (BETA only; v1.0 rejects it)
  ↓  POST (encrypted) to notificationUrl
apps/web/app/api/teams/notifications/route.ts   (the "always-on listener", on Vercel)
  • validation handshake (echo ?validationToken — Graph checks this at create time)
  • verify clientState, decrypt the rich payload (graph-notification-crypto.ts:
    RSA-OAEP unwrap → HMAC-SHA256 verify → AES-256-CBC decrypt)
  • triggerTask('teams-transcript-ingestion', { transcriptContentUrl, meetingId, ... })
  ↓
apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-transcript-ingestion.ts
  • fetch the WebVTT (graph-transcripts.ts:fetchTranscriptVtt)
  • parseVtt → mergeBySpeaker → one utterance per turn
  • resolve speaker → employee by display-name (null when unmatched)
  • INSERT a channel (per call) + channel_participants + messages
    (role='user', extraction_status='pending', clientMessageId='teams:<transcriptId>:<n>')
  ↓
existing claim-extraction cron picks up the pending messages  →  candidate-before-claim
```

Each utterance becomes a `messages` row (not a `document_chunk`) precisely so it carries speaker attribution (`employeeId`) and verbatim quotes — the same evidence shape as chat. Idempotent: re-running for the same transcript is a no-op (the `clientMessageId` dedupe key). Nothing here writes to `claims` — the normal R5/R6 validators run downstream.

Subscription lifecycle is owned by `teams-subscription-manager.ts`: the `teams-subscription-renew` cron (`*/30`) and a webhook-lifecycle-triggered task both call the idempotent `ensureAdhocSubscription()` (renew if <20 min left, else create). The resource max-lifetime is ~1h, so a machine must keep re-upping it — no human re-authenticates anything.

**Hard constraints (derived from Microsoft Graph, not choices):**
- **Ad-hoc calls are only reachable via a subscription, and only on beta.** `getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId=...)` (used by `diagnose-transcripts.ps1`) enumerates *scheduled* meetings only; ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls never appear there. The subscription is "listen going forward" — a transcript only notifies if the subscription existed *before* transcription started; past calls are unrecoverable.
- **No Graph live transcript / no Graph live spoken awareness.** Graph exposes no API to read a meeting's live caption/transcript stream, and Teams does not pipe spoken words into the meeting text chat. The Graph path therefore ingests calls *after* they end. Live spoken participation is a separate Recall.ai meeting-bot path, not a Graph capability.
- **The webhook must be deployed before the subscription can be created** — Graph validates `notificationUrl` synchronously at create time.

The Graph subscription/transcript surface is intentionally duplicated: `apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts` (web/webhook side) and `apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts` (worker side), because apps/web and apps/workers are separate processes and cross-app imports aren't allowed. The web copy is the reference. See AGENTS.md §10.

**Status: LIVE + validated end-to-end (2026-06-04/05).** Real call → 95 speaker-attributed messages → claims. Refinements made while validating on real data:

- **Speaker resolution is email-based.** VTT display name → M365 directory email (Graph `/users`) → employee by `employees.email` or `employee_identities.email`; an unmatched `@popcre.com` speaker bootstraps a provisional employee. (`employees.name` often differs from the transcript display name, so name-matching alone failed.)

input.new_string:
• INSERT a channel (per call) + channel_participants + messages
    (role='user', extraction_status='awaiting_approval', clientMessageId='teams:<transcriptId>:<n>')
  ↓
admin reviews at /admin/transcripts → Approve flips the channel's messages to 'pending'
  ↓
existing claim-extraction cron picks up the pending messages  →  candidate-before-claim
```

**Approval gate (2026-06-24):** ingested utterances land `extraction_status='awaiting_approval'`, which the claim-extraction cron never selects, so a transcript produces no claims until an admin approves it at `/admin/transcripts` (approve → `pending`; reject → `skipped`). `raw_transcripts.approval_status` records the decision. See DECISIONS.md `D-transcript-approval-gate`.

Subscription lifecycle is owned by `teams-subscription-manager.ts`: the `teams-subscription-renew` cron (`*/30`) and a webhook-lifecycle-triggered task both call the idempotent `ensureAdhocSubscription()` (renew if <20 min left, else create). **Hard constraints (derived from Microsoft Graph, not choices):**
- **Ad-hoc calls are only reachable via a subscription, and only on beta.** `getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId=...)` (used by `diagnose-transcripts.ps1`) enumerates *scheduled* meetings only; ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls never appear there. input.new_string:
Subscription lifecycle is owned by `teams-subscription-manager.ts`: the `teams-subscription-renew` cron (`*/30`) and a webhook-lifecycle-triggered task both call the idempotent `ensureAllSubscriptions()`, which keeps BOTH transcript subscriptions alive (renew if <20 min left, else create; a benign "limit of 1" 403 on create is treated as already-exists → renew). The resource max-lifetime is ~1h, so a machine must keep re-upping it — no human re-authenticates anything. Reuses the single existing 30-min schedule for both resources (Trigger.dev is at its 10/10 schedule limit — AGENTS.md §10).

**Hard constraints (derived from Microsoft Graph, not choices):**
- **Live capture is "listen going forward."** A subscription only notifies for transcripts produced *after* it existed; calls during a subscription gap aren't pushed. To recover already-completed *scheduled* transcripts, the on-demand `teams-transcript-backfill` task enumerates organizers and pulls `getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId=...,startDateTime=...)` (the same enumeration `diagnose-transcripts.ps1` probes), then triggers ingestion per transcript (idempotent). Ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls are NOT enumerable that way — they are recoverable only via their live subscription.

file.content:
<#
  diagnose-transcripts.ps1
  Tries several ways of asking Microsoft for Albert's transcripts and reports
  exactly what each returns. Read-only. Reads creds from ..\.env.local
#>
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

function Get-Val([string]$key) {
    $envFile = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot '..\.env.local'
    $line = Get-Content $envFile | Where-Object { $_ -match "^\s*$key\s*=" } | Select-Object -First 1
    if ($line) { return ($line -replace "^\s*$key\s*=\s*", '').Trim().Trim('"').Trim("'") }
    return $null
}

$tenantId = Get-Val 'AZURE_TENANT_ID'
$clientId = Get-Val 'AZURE_GRAPH_CLIENT_ID'
$secret   = Get-Val 'AZURE_GRAPH_CLIENT_SECRET'

$tok = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post `
    -Uri ("https://login.microsoftonline.com/{0}/oauth2/v2.0/token" -f $tenantId) `
    -ContentType 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' `
    -Body @{ client_id=$clientId; client_secret=$secret; scope='https://graph.microsoft.com/.default'; grant_type='client_credentials' }
$H = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $($tok.access_token)" }

$u = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Get -Headers $H -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/Albert@popcre.com'
$uid = $u.id
"Organizer: $($u.displayName)  id=$uid"
""

function Try-Url([string]$label, [string]$url) {
    Write-Host ("=== {0} ===" -f $label) -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host ("    {0}" -f $url) -ForegroundColor DarkGray
    try {
        $r = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Get -Headers $H -Uri $url
        $items = @($r.value)
        Write-Host ("    HTTP 200  - {0} item(s)" -f $items.Count) -ForegroundColor Green
        foreach ($it in $items) {
            Write-Host ("      - created={0}  meetingId={1}" -f $it.createdDateTime, $it.meetingId)
        }
    } catch {
        $code = $null; try { $code = [int]$_.Exception.Response.StatusCode } catch {}
        Write-Host ("    HTTP {0}" -f $code) -ForegroundColor Yellow
        try { if ($_.ErrorDetails.Message) { Write-Host ("    {0}" -f ($_.ErrorDetails.Message -replace '\s+',' ')) -ForegroundColor DarkYellow } } catch {}
    }
    ""
}

$start = '2026-06-01T00:00:00.0000000Z'
$end   = '2026-06-04T00:00:00.0000000Z'

Try-Url 'v1.0 bare' `
  "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/$uid/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='$uid')"

Try-Url 'v1.0 with date window' `
  "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/$uid/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='$uid',startDateTime=$start,endDateTime=$end)"

Try-Url 'beta bare' `
  "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/users/$uid/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='$uid')"

Try-Url 'beta with date window' `
  "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/users/$uid/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(meetingOrganizerUserId='$uid',startDateTime=$start,endDateTime=$end)"

content.text: Found it. The repo's diagnostic uses **`users/{uid}/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(...)`** — but my backfill helper used **`communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(...)`** (the *subscription* resource, not the *pull* endpoint). That wrong path would 404 for every organizer, which my helper silently swallows as "no transcripts" → 0 dispatched. Let me confirm the correct endpoint with a read-only probe before fixing + redeploying, so I don't guess twice.

content.text: The `AZURE_*` creds live in the Trigger.dev project env, not `.env.local`, so I can't probe locally. But the repo's own diagnostic script is authoritative: the correct path is `users/{id}/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(...)`, not `communications/...`. Let me fix the helper (correct endpoint, add `endDateTime`, and surface `403` as an error instead of silently swallowing — so a tenant access-policy gap becomes visible rather than looking like "no transcripts").

content.content: The file C:\repos\oracle\apps\workers\src\lib\graph-transcripts.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -      // Per-organizer best-effort: skip users with no meetings / no access.
+      // 404 = this organizer simply has no transcripts in the window (expected
+      // for most users). Anything else (esp. 403 = app-access-policy gap) is a
+      // real signal — surface it as an error rather than masking it as "empty".
+      throw new Error(`getAllTranscripts failed (${res.status}) for organizer ${organizerId}: ${(await res.text()).slice(0, 300)}`);

content.subject: Claims grouped by meeting/document
content.description: Extend the LATERAL evidence subquery in apps/web/app/admin/claims/page.tsx to return source_group_key + source_group_label (channel/document/external/manual), group rows, render group header rows.

content.subject: Shift-click range selection on checkboxes
content.description: Add data attributes to checkboxes and a 'use client' shift-select.tsx enhancer that toggles ranges within the same form group.

content.subject: Transcript approval gate: migrations + enum
content.description: Add 'awaiting_approval' to extractionStatusEnum (schema.ts) + generate Drizzle migration. Hand-written 75_transcript_approval.sql adding approval columns to raw_transcripts with one-time backfill to 'approved'.

content.subject: Ingestion worker holds transcripts
content.description: teams-transcript-ingestion.ts: insert messages with extractionStatus 'awaiting_approval' instead of 'pending'.

content.subject: /admin/transcripts page + actions + nav
content.description: New server page listing raw_transcripts with approve/reject server actions (admin-guarded) flipping message extraction_status; add Transcripts nav link in Activity group.

content.subject: Verify build, UI, and gate behavior
content.description: Run web build/typecheck, verify grouping + shift-click in preview, verify gate holds/approves/rejects at DB level, check backfill. Update AGENTS.md rows.

content.subject: Generalize Graph subscription helper
content.description: graph-transcripts.ts: extract ensureSubscription(resource), add ONLINE_MEETINGS_RESOURCE + ensureAllSubscriptions(), harden 403 limit race, add listUsers().

content.subject: Manager ensures both subscriptions
content.description: teams-subscription-manager.ts runEnsure calls ensureAllSubscriptions(); reuse existing cron + lifecycle task (no new schedule).

content.subject: Backfill worker for missed transcripts
content.description: New teams-transcript-backfill.ts task: enumerate organizers, getAllTranscripts over a window, trigger ingestion per transcript (idempotent).

content.subject: Sync web Graph helper + webhook comment + docs
content.description: apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts add onlineMeetings resource + parameterize create; webhook header comment; AGENTS.md/architecture/DECISIONS docs.

content.subject: Typecheck, deploy, verify subscriptions + backfill
content.description: Typecheck web+workers, deploy worker, confirm both subscriptions live via Trigger MCP, run backfill, verify Jun 23 transcript lands in /admin/transcripts (read-only DB check).

content.text: The transcript was fetched and parsed fine (real content in the params — "I get it. It's very grim dark…"), but the **`messages` insert failed**. The telling detail: current prod *has* the `awaiting_approval` enum value (I verified it), so if the worker were writing to current prod this insert would succeed. A failure here strongly implies **the worker is writing to a different database — `oracle.old`** — which never got the gate migration (so `awaiting_approval` is an invalid enum there). That's the cutover miss AGENTS.md §10 warned about. Let me confirm by checking both DBs.

content.text: Surprise — the worker **is** writing to current prod (it now has 2 `raw_transcripts` rows + 7 Teams-call channels from today; `oracle.old` is untouched). So it's not a wrong-DB problem, and prod *does* have the enum value. That means the `messages` insert is failing for another reason — and the prime suspect is a **CHECK constraint** that enumerates the allowed `extraction_status` values separately from the enum type (I added the enum value but a check constraint may not list it). Let me look.

content.text: Found the real, systemic cause. Look at the `client_message_id` in the params: `teams:MiMjMWIyMzMwZDQx…Ng==:0` — the online-meeting transcript content URL yields a **~230-char transcript id**, so `client_message_id` blows past **`varchar(255)`**. The ad-hoc path always had short ids, so it never hit this. Both runs failed identically → systemic. The fix: hash an over-long derived transcript id down to a stable short value (the full id is still kept in `metadata_json`). Let me read the exact function and fix it.

content.content: The file C:\repos\oracle\apps\workers\src\trigger\teams-transcript-ingestion.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +    // Scheduled online-meeting transcript ids are long base64 blobs (~230
+    // (varchar(255)), so hash anything long to a stable 32-char digest. Short
+    // ad-hoc ids pass through unchanged (preserves existing dedup keys). The
+    // full id is still kept verbatim in messages.metadata_json.transcriptId.

content.text:
It works end-to-end. Here's the verified prod state:

- **371 scheduled-meeting utterances ingested**, all `awaiting_approval` — across 2 transcripts (241 + 130 utterances) recovered by the backfill. These are now waiting in **`/admin/transcripts`** for your approval. (The 95 `complete` are the old Jun 4 ad-hoc call.)
- The scheduled-meeting **subscription is live** and the manager keeps both subscriptions renewed.

To get there I had to fix **two bugs** the verification surfaced (both now deployed as `v20260624.3`):
1. **Wrong pull endpoint** — backfill used `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts` (the *subscription* resource); the *pull* endpoint is `users/{id}/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts`. Silent 0 results.
2. **`client_message_id` overflow** — scheduled-meeting transcript ids are ~230-char base64 blobs; `teams:<id>:<n>` exceeded `varchar(255)`, failing every `messages` insert. Now hashed to 32 chars (full id still kept in `metadata_json`).

Two loose ends from the debugging:

message.content: the system is not supposed to automatically ingest every meeting. i have to see a list of meetings and choose which to ingest

content.text:
You're right, and that's a real misread on my part — thank you for catching it. I built the gate at the **extraction** stage (auto-ingest *every* meeting's transcript, then you approve which become claims). What you want is a gate at the **ingestion** stage: a **list of meetings → you pick which ones get pulled in at all**. Nothing should be ingested without your choice.

That also means the system as currently deployed is doing the wrong thing: the live `onlineMeetings` subscription **auto-ingests every scheduled meeting's transcript** into the DB (held from extraction, but still pulled in). I'll change that — but let me get the design right with two questions first.

questions.question: How should the 'list of meetings to choose from' be populated?

options.label: Auto-discover (metadata only)
options.description: Keep a subscription, but it only RECORDS that a meeting/transcript exists (title, date, organizer, participants) — it does NOT pull the transcript content. The list fills in automatically as meetings happen; you pick which to actually ingest. Recommended.

options.label: On-demand scan
options.description: No standing subscription. You click 'Scan for meetings' (or pick a date range) and I query Graph for available transcripts and show the list. Nothing is recorded or pulled until you choose. Simpler, but the list is only as fresh as your last scan.

questions.question: When you choose a meeting and ingest it, what happens to claims?

options.label: Ingest → auto-extract
options.description: Choosing to ingest IS the go-ahead: the transcript is pulled in and flows straight into normal claim extraction (claims still go through the existing per-claim review). Drops the separate 'awaiting_approval' transcript gate.

options.label: Ingest → still hold
options.description: Keep the two-step gate: ingesting pulls the transcript in but holds it; you separately approve it for extraction on /admin/transcripts. More control, two clicks.

questions.question: What about the 2 meetings (372 utterances) I already auto-ingested into the queue?

options.label: Delete them
options.description: Remove those auto-ingested transcripts + their messages/channels from prod, so truly nothing is ingested except what you explicitly choose going forward.

options.label: Keep them
options.description: Leave them in the queue as the first items for you to approve/reject — they include yesterday's meeting.

Here are the two meetings the backfill pulled in (both within the last 14 days). Note: the timestamps in the names are *ingestion* time, not meeting time (a worker limitation), so use the participants to identify them:

**Meeting 1 — 241 utterances**
Participants: **Anthony Oliveros, Albert H., Jessica Cortazar, Alejandra Pinilla**

Participants: **Ilona Kereki, Vaibhav Barot, Uma Meka** (+ 1 unknown speaker)
Opening: *"It's very grim dark… post-apocalyptic… dystopian but a bit more agrarian and nicer."*

Do you want me to **delete both** (so nothing is ingested except what you explicitly pick once the new flow exists), or **keep** either/both?

---

On the redesign: based on your answers, the target flow is now clear and it changes what I built:
- **Subscription becomes discovery-only** — record meeting metadata (title/date/organizer/participants) when a transcript becomes available; **don't pull content**.
- **`/admin/transcripts` becomes a meeting picker** — a list of discovered meetings with an **Ingest** button per meeting (and bulk-select).
- **Ingesting = the go-ahead** — picking a meeting pulls its transcript and sends it straight to normal extraction (dropping the separate `awaiting_approval` hold I built).

That's a meaningful rework (new discovery table, webhook no longer auto-ingests, picker UI, repurpose the backfill into a metadata scan). I'll put it in plan mode and lay out the concrete plan for your approval before changing anything. Want me to go ahead and draft that plan? And — keep or delete those two meetings?

content.text:
Yes — **as long as the redesign's discovery includes a scan of recent past meetings, not just the going-forward subscription.** Here's the precise picture:

- **Deleting Meeting 1 doesn't destroy the transcript** — it only removes the copy we pulled into our DB. The actual transcript still lives in Microsoft Graph (subject to your Teams retention policy), and we already proved it's fetchable.
- **The subscription alone won't resurface it** — subscriptions only "listen going forward," so a meeting that already happened never re-notifies.
- **A discovery scan will** — the same `getAllTranscripts` enumeration the backfill used can list *already-completed* meetings as metadata. So if I build discovery to run that scan (repurposed from the backfill, metadata-only), Meeting 1 shows up in the picker as "available — not yet ingested," and you choose whether to pull it in.

So the plan needs **two discovery sources**: the subscription (new meetings, real-time) **and** a recent-window scan (so past meetings like these two appear too). I'll make that explicit in the plan.

Net: it's safe to delete both now — they'll come back as *pickable* items (not auto-ingested) once the picker ships, and you decide then. The only caveat is Teams retention: a meeting that ages out of Graph's retention can't be re-fetched, but these are recent, so that's not a concern here.

Want me to **delete both now and draft the redesign plan** (discovery = subscription + recent-window scan → meeting picker → ingest-on-choice → auto-extract)?
